content:
# workflow-gstack-agentic-development-workflow - Overview

Workflow type: agentic_software_delivery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Establish a Think→Plan→Build→Review→Test→Ship→Reflect 7-stage software delivery pipeline for AI-assisted development, replacing single-prompt code generation with disciplined engineering workflow.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

When to use:
- Starting a new feature or project
- Large refactoring requiring architecture review
- Web projects needing browser QA
- Multi-agent collaboration projects

Inputs required:
- User task description
- Project constraints and platform
- Domain knowledge from /brief or rules

AI summary: Think-Plan-Build-Review-Test-Ship-Reflect 7-stage agentic software delivery pipeline for AI-assisted development. Agent must go through all stages instead of jumping directly to code. Each stage has stop conditions and review gates.

prompt_tags: Think Plan Build Review Test Ship Reflect

content:
# workflow-gstack-agentic-development-workflow - Execution Steps

Workflow type: agentic_software_delivery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Establish a Think→Plan→Build→Review→Test→Ship→Reflect 7-stage software delivery pipeline for AI-assisted development, replacing single-prompt code generation with disciplined engineering workflow.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Think: redefine the problem, answer 6 mandatory questions
- Plan: design architecture, data flow, test matrix; pass required review gates
- Build: implement per plan, write tests, checkpoint commits
- Review: code review, design audit, DX audit, security audit (as applicable)
- Test: browser QA, regression tests, security scan
- Ship: sync main→test→push→PR→merge→deploy→canary
- Reflect: write retro, update rules/checklists/templates in database

AI summary: Think-Plan-Build-Review-Test-Ship-Reflect 7-stage agentic software delivery pipeline for AI-assisted development. Agent must go through all stages instead of jumping directly to code. Each stage has stop conditions and review gates.

prompt_tags: Think Plan Build Review Test Ship Reflect

content:
# workflow-gstack-agentic-development-workflow - Prompt Contract

Workflow type: agentic_software_delivery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Establish a Think→Plan→Build→Review→Test→Ship→Reflect 7-stage software delivery pipeline for AI-assisted development, replacing single-prompt code generation with disciplined engineering workflow.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Agent must not skip from Think to Build without Plan
- Each stage has a stop condition that must be met
- Output of each stage feeds the next stage
- User has final say over AI recommendations (User Sovereignty)

AI summary: Think-Plan-Build-Review-Test-Ship-Reflect 7-stage agentic software delivery pipeline for AI-assisted development. Agent must go through all stages instead of jumping directly to code. Each stage has stop conditions and review gates.

prompt_tags: Think Plan Build Review Test Ship Reflect

content:
# workflow-gstack-agentic-development-workflow - Failure Modes

Workflow type: agentic_software_delivery
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Establish a Think→Plan→Build→Review→Test→Ship→Reflect 7-stage software delivery pipeline for AI-assisted development, replacing single-prompt code generation with disciplined engineering workflow.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Agent jumps directly to Build without Think/Plan
- Skipping tests because it seems to work
- Ignoring review gate failures to proceed faster
- Not writing retro after completion

AI summary: Think-Plan-Build-Review-Test-Ship-Reflect 7-stage agentic software delivery pipeline for AI-assisted development. Agent must go through all stages instead of jumping directly to code. Each stage has stop conditions and review gates.
